iterations.llm_response:
<lean>
axiom obj : Type

-- Predicates
axiom InClub : obj → Prop
axiom PerformsOften : obj → Prop
axiom AttendsEngaged : obj → Prop
axiom InactiveDisinterested : obj → Prop
axiom ChaperoneDances : obj → Prop
axiom StudentAtSchool : obj → Prop
axiom YoungOrTeen : obj → Prop
axiom WishAcademic : obj → Prop

-- Axioms from the context
-- 1. In-club and performs often -> attends and is very engaged
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ PerformsOften x → AttendsEngaged x
-- 2. In-club -> performs often or inactive/disinterested
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x → PerformsOften x ∨ InactiveDisinterested x
-- 3. In-club and chaperone -> not a student at the school
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ ChaperoneDances x → ¬ StudentAtSchool x
-- 4. In-club and inactive/disinterested -> chaperone dances
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ InactiveDisinterested x → ChaperoneDances x
-- 5. In-club and young/teen and wishes academic -> student at school
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ YoungOrTeen x ∧ WishAcademic x → StudentAtSchool x

-- Bonnie and her specific information
axiom Bonnie : obj
axiom Bonnie_info :
  InClub Bonnie ∧
  ((AttendsEngaged Bonnie ∧ StudentAtSchool Bonnie) ∨
   (¬ AttendsEngaged Bonnie ∧ ¬ StudentAtSchool Bonnie))

-- Useful derived facts
lemma bonnie_in_club : InClub Bonnie := And.left Bonnie_info

lemma bonnie_equiv_disj :
  (AttendsEngaged Bonnie ∧ StudentAtSchool Bonnie) ∨
  (¬ AttendsEngaged Bonnie ∧ ¬ StudentAtSchool Bonnie) :=
And.right Bonnie_info

-- If Bonnie is a student, then she performs often
lemma student_implies_performs :
  StudentAtSchool Bonnie → PerformsOften Bonnie :=
by
  intro hS
  -- Show not inactive, otherwise contradiction with student via chaperoning
  have hNotInactive : ¬ InactiveDisinterested Bonnie := by
    intro hInact
    have hCh : ChaperoneDances Bonnie :=
      A4 Bonnie (And.intro bonnie_in_club hInact)
    have hNotS : ¬ StudentAtSchool Bonnie :=
      A3 Bonnie (And.intro bonnie_in_club hCh)
    exact hNotS hS
  -- From in-club, either performs or inactive; exclude inactive
  have h := A2 Bonnie bonnie_in_club
  cases h with
  | inl hPerf => exact hPerf
  | inr hInact => exact False.elim (hNotInactive hInact)

-- If Bonnie is not attending/engaged, then she does not perform often
lemma not_engaged_implies_not_performs :
  ¬ AttendsEngaged Bonnie → ¬ PerformsOften Bonnie :=
by
  intro hNotEng
  intro hPerf
  have hEng : AttendsEngaged Bonnie :=
    A1 Bonnie (And.intro bonnie_in_club hPerf)
  exact hNotEng hEng

-- From Bonnie's disjunction, we can only conclude a case split on performing
theorem bonnie_performs_or_not : PerformsOften Bonnie ∨ ¬ PerformsOften Bonnie :=
by
  have h := bonnie_equiv_disj
  cases h with
  | inl h1 =>
      have hS : StudentAtSchool Bonnie := And.right h1
      exact Or.inl (student_implies_performs hS)
  | inr h2 =>
      have hNotEng : ¬ AttendsEngaged Bonnie := And.left h2
      exact Or.inr (not_engaged_implies_not_performs hNotEng)
</lean>

ANSWER: Failure

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom Bonnie : obj

-- Predicates
axiom InClub : obj → Prop
axiom PerformOften : obj → Prop
axiom AttendsEngaged : obj → Prop
axiom Inactive : obj → Prop
axiom Chaperone : obj → Prop
axiom Student : obj → Prop
axiom Youth : obj → Prop
axiom Wishes : obj → Prop

-- Premises
-- 1. Club members who perform often attend and are very engaged with school events.
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ PerformOften x → AttendsEngaged x
-- 2. Club members either perform often or are inactive and disinterested.
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x → (PerformOften x ∨ Inactive x)
-- 3. Club members who chaperone dances are not students who attend the school.
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ Chaperone x → ¬ Student x
-- 4. Club members who are inactive and disinterested chaperone dances.
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ Inactive x → Chaperone x
-- 5. Young children/teenagers in the club who wish to further academics are students.
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ Youth x ∧ Wishes x → Student x
-- 6. Bonnie is in the club.
axiom B_in : InClub Bonnie
-- 7. Bonnie either (attends&engaged and is a student) or (not attends&engaged and not a student).
axiom B_xnor : (AttendsEngaged Bonnie ∧ Student Bonnie) ∨ (¬ AttendsEngaged Bonnie ∧ ¬ Student Bonnie)

-- Useful equivalence from Bonnie's XNOR: AttendsEngaged ↔ Student
lemma AE_iff_S : AttendsEngaged Bonnie ↔ Student Bonnie := by
  apply Iff.intro
  · intro hAE
    cases B_xnor with
    | inl h =>
        exact h.right
    | inr h =>
        have : False := h.left hAE
        exact False.elim this
  · intro hS
    cases B_xnor with
    | inl h =>
        exact h.left
    | inr h =>
        have : False := h.right hS
        exact False.elim this

-- The antecedent (Bonnie chaperones or, if not, she performs often) holds from the premises.
lemma antecedent_true :
  (Chaperone Bonnie ∨ (¬ Chaperone Bonnie → PerformOften Bonnie)) := by
  have h := P2 Bonnie B_in
  cases h with
  | inl hPerf =>
      -- If she performs often, then (¬Ch → Perf) holds.
      exact Or.inr (fun _ => hPerf)
  | inr hInact =>
      -- If she is inactive, she chaperones.
      have hCh : Chaperone Bonnie := P4 Bonnie (And.intro B_in hInact)
      exact Or.inl hCh

-- The conclusion's RHS is impossible under the premises.
lemma conclusion_impossible :
  ¬ ((Youth Bonnie ∧ Wishes Bonnie) ∧ Inactive Bonnie) := by
  intro h
  have hYW : Youth Bonnie ∧ Wishes Bonnie := h.left
  have hIn : Inactive Bonnie := h.right
  have hCh : Chaperone Bonnie := P4 Bonnie (And.intro B_in hIn)
  have hNotS : ¬ Student Bonnie := P3 Bonnie (And.intro B_in hCh)
  have hS : Student Bonnie := P5 Bonnie (And.intro B_in (And.intro hYW.left hYW.right))
  exact hNotS hS

-- Therefore, the implication in the statement is false under the premises.
theorem statement_false :
  ¬ ((Chaperone Bonnie ∨ (¬ Chaperone Bonnie → PerformOften Bonnie)) →
     ((Youth Bonnie ∧ Wishes Bonnie) ∧ Inactive Bonnie)) := by
  intro hImp
  have hA : (Chaperone Bonnie ∨ (¬ Chaperone Bonnie → PerformOften Bonnie)) := antecedent_true
  have hB : (Youth Bonnie ∧ Wishes Bonnie) ∧ Inactive Bonnie := hImp hA
  exact conclusion_impossible hB
</lean>

axiom Shape : obj → Prop

-- No brutalist buildings that Max adores have interesting geometries.
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, Brutalist x ∧ Adores x → ¬ Interesting x
-- Every style that Max adores is either Zaha Hadid's design style or Kelly Wearstler's design style.
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Adores x → Zaha x ∨ Kelly x
-- All of Kelly Wearstler's design styles that Max adores are evocative.
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Kelly x ∧ Adores x → Evocative x
-- All of Kelly Wearstler's design styles that Max adores are dreamy.
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, Kelly x ∧ Adores x → Dreamy x
-- If a design by Max that he adores has interesting geometries, then it is a brutalist building and evocative.
axiom P6 : ∀ x : obj, Adores x ∧ Interesting x → Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x

-- Conclusion: Any design that Max adores is evocative and dreamy.

  intro x hxA
  -- First, show that nothing Max adores can be interesting.
  have hNotI : ¬ Interesting x :=

      intro hxI
      have hBE : Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x := P6 x ⟨hxA, hxI⟩
      have hB : Brutalist x := And.left hBE
      have hNI : ¬ Interesting x := P2 x ⟨hB, hxA⟩
      exact hNI hxI
  -- From adored, x is either Zaha or Kelly; exclude Zaha using hNotI.
  have hz_or_k : Zaha x ∨ Kelly x := P3 x hxA
  have hK : Kelly x :=

      cases hz_or_k with

  have hD : Dreamy x := P5 x ⟨hK, hxA⟩

| a => True
| b => False

| a => True
| b => False

| a => True
| b => True

  intro x
  cases x with
  | a =>
      intro _; exact True.intro
  | b =>
      intro hb; cases hb

  refine ⟨b, ?_, ?_⟩
  · exact True.intro
  · intro hM; cases hM

-- In this model, there is a pet that is a cat, falsifying "No pets are cats".
theorem model_exists_pet_cat : ∃ x : U, PetM x ∧ CatM x := by
  exact ⟨a, True.intro, True.intro⟩

axiom Cow : obj → Prop

axiom Alligator : obj → Prop

-- Premises
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, BrownSwiss x → Cow x                  -- All Brown Swiss cattle are cows.

axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Cow x → Domesticated x                -- All cows are domesticated animals.
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Alligator x → ¬ Domesticated x        -- Alligators are not domesticated animals.
axiom P5 : Alligator Ted                                    -- Ted is an alligator.

-- Consequence: Ted is not domesticated.
theorem ted_not_domesticated : ¬ Domesticated Ted := by
  have hAll : Alligator Ted := P5
  exact P4 Ted hAll

-- Consequence: There exists a pet that is domesticated (from P2, P1, P3).
theorem exists_pet_domesticated : ∃ x : obj, Pet x ∧ Domesticated x := by
  cases P2 with
  | intro x hx =>
    have hxPet : Pet x := And.left hx
    have hxBS  : BrownSwiss x := And.right hx
    have hxCow : Cow x := P1 x hxBS
    have hxDom : Domesticated x := P3 x hxCow
    exact Exists.intro x (And.intro hxPet hxDom)
</lean>

axiom MachineTranslation : obj

axiom NLPTask : obj → Prop
axiom LanguageGenerationTask : obj → Prop
axiom LanguageUnderstandingTask : obj → Prop
axiom OutputIsTextSequence : obj → Prop

-- A natural language processing task can be categorized as either generation or understanding.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, NLPTask x → (LanguageGenerationTask x ∨ LanguageUnderstandingTask x)

-- If the output of a natural language processing task is a text sequence, then it is a language generation task.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, NLPTask x → OutputIsTextSequence x → LanguageGenerationTask x

-- Machine translation is a natural language processing task.
axiom A3 : NLPTask MachineTranslation

-- The output of machine translation is a text sequence.
axiom A4 : OutputIsTextSequence MachineTranslation

theorem mt_is_language_generation_task : LanguageGenerationTask MachineTranslation := by
  exact A2 MachineTranslation A3 A4

<lean>
axiom Building : Type
axiom Person : Type
axiom Animal : Type

axiom Managed : Building → Prop
axiom AllowsPets : Building → Prop
axiom Pet : Animal → Prop
axiom Cat : Animal → Prop
axiom OwnerOf : Person → Animal → Prop

axiom AllowedWith : Person → Animal → Building → Prop
axiom WillRentIn : Person → Building → Prop

axiom Rent : Building → Nat
axiom Deposit : Building → Nat

axiom Tom : Person
axiom Fluffy : Animal
axiom OliveGarden : Building

-- Facts from the context
axiom Fluffy_is_cat : Cat Fluffy
axiom cats_are_pets : ∀ a : Animal, Cat a → Pet a
axiom OliveGarden_managed : Managed OliveGarden
axiom rent_OG_2000 : Rent OliveGarden = 2000
axiom two_thousand_gt_one_thousand_five_hundred : 1500 < 2000
axiom deposit_at_least_rent : ∀ b : Building, Managed b → Deposit b ≥ Rent b
axiom some_managed_allow_pets : ∃ b : Building, Managed b ∧ AllowsPets b
axiom if_allows_pets_then_people_allowed_with_pet :
  ∀ (p : Person) (a : Animal) (b : Building), Managed b → AllowsPets b → Pet a → AllowedWith p a b
axiom tom_rents_if_allowed_and_deposit_small :
  ∀ b : Building, Managed b → (AllowedWith Tom Fluffy b ∧ Deposit b ≤ 1500) → WillRentIn Tom b

-- Derivation: at Olive Garden, the deposit is strictly greater than 1500
theorem deposit_gt_1500_OG : Deposit OliveGarden > 1500 := by
  have h1 : Rent OliveGarden ≤ Deposit OliveGarden := deposit_at_least_rent OliveGarden OliveGarden_managed
  have h1' : 2000 ≤ Deposit OliveGarden := by
    simpa [rent_OG_2000] using h1
  have h2 : 1500 < Deposit OliveGarden := lt_of_lt_of_le two_thousand_gt_one_thousand_five_hundred h1'
  exact h2

theorem deposit_not_le_1500_OG : ¬ Deposit OliveGarden ≤ 1500 := by
  exact not_le_of_gt deposit_gt_1500_OG

-- Hence Tom's sufficient condition to rent at the Olive Garden fails
theorem tom_condition_for_OG_fails :
  ¬ (AllowedWith Tom Fluffy OliveGarden ∧ Deposit OliveGarden ≤ 1500) := by
  intro h
  exact deposit_not_le_1500_OG h.right
</lean>

-- All animals with backbones reproduce by male-and-female mating

-- All vertebrate animals have a backbone
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Vertebrate x → Backbone x
-- All bees do not reproduce by male-and-female mating

-- All queen bees are bees

  have notRMF : ¬ ReproduceMF Harry := A4 Harry beeH

  have invH : Invertebrate Harry :=
    Or.elim inv_or_vert
      (fun inv => inv)
      (fun vert =>
        have bb : Backbone Harry := A3 Harry vert
        have rmf : ReproduceMF Harry := A2 Harry bb
        False.elim (notRMF rmf)
      )
  exact Or.inl invH

axiom Vertebrate : obj → Prop

axiom Bee : obj → Prop
axiom QueenBee : obj → Prop

  A4 Harry A6

theorem harry_not_backbone : ¬ Backbone Harry :=
  fun hb => harry_not_mf (A2 Harry hb)

theorem harry_not_vertebrate : ¬ Vertebrate Harry :=
  fun hv => harry_not_backbone (A3 Harry hv)

-- The antecedent of the statement holds (right disjunct).
theorem antecedent_holds :
  (Vertebrate Harry ∧ Backbone Harry) ∨ (¬ Vertebrate Harry ∧ ¬ Backbone Harry) :=
by
  right
  exact And.intro harry_not_vertebrate harry_not_backbone

axiom SpendTimeWithSiblings : obj → Prop

axiom LiveAtHome : obj → Prop
axiom LiveWithStrangers : obj → Prop

axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, MultiBirth x → SpendTimeWithSiblings x
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, HaveSibsBornTogether x → MultiBirth x
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, ComplainOftenAboutAnnoyingSiblings x → HaveSibsBornTogether x
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, LiveAtHome x → ¬ LiveWithStrangers x

  have hhome : LiveAtHome Luke := A5 Luke hsp
  have hnstr : ¬ LiveWithStrangers Luke := A4 Luke hhome
  exact hnstr hs

  cases A7 with
  | inl h =>
      exact False.elim (luke_not_left_disjunct h)

      exact h

  And.right luke_right_disjunct

  have hHome : LivesAtHome x := P5 x hST

  have hconj := luke_not_mb_and_not_lws
  cases hconj with
  | intro hnotMB hnotLWS =>
      exact hnotMB hMB

  cases A8 with

  cases hcat with

-- Heck cattle were bred back in the 1920s to resemble the aurochs

-- Heck cattle are animals
axiom Heck_are_animals : ∀ x : obj, HeckCattle x → Animal x

by
  apply Exists.elim Heck_bred_back_resemble_aurochs
  intro x hx
  apply And.elim hx
  intro hxHeck hrest
  apply And.elim hrest
  intro hxBred hxRes
  have hAS : ArtificiallySelected x := BB_is_AS x hxBred
  exact Exists.intro x (And.intro hxHeck hAS)
</lean>

axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Organic x → Chemical x
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Organic x → ContainsCarbon x

axiom P6 : (Chemical Mixture ∧ SingleElement Mixture) ∨ (¬ Chemical Mixture ∧ ¬ SingleElement Mixture)

-- From the chain Alkane → Hydrocarbon → Organic → Chemical
lemma alkane_to_chemical : ∀ x : obj, Alkane x → Chemical x := by
  intro x
  intro hA
  have hH : Hydrocarbon x := P2 x hA
  have hO : Organic x := P1 x hH
  have hC : Chemical x := P3 x hO
  exact hC

-- From the exclusivity in P5 and the equivalence-style P6, Mixture is not a chemical compound
lemma not_chemical_mixture : ¬ Chemical Mixture := by
  cases P6 with
  | inl h =>
      have hc : Chemical Mixture := h.left
      have hs : SingleElement Mixture := h.right
      have hns : ¬ SingleElement Mixture := P5 Mixture hc
      exact hns hs
  | inr h =>
      exact h.left

lemma not_singleelement_mixture : ¬ SingleElement Mixture := by
  cases P6 with
  | inl h =>
      have hc : Chemical Mixture := h.left
      have hs : SingleElement Mixture := h.right
      have hns : ¬ SingleElement Mixture := P5 Mixture hc
      exact hns hs
  | inr h =>
      exact h.right

-- Hence Mixture cannot be an alkane (since alkanes are chemicals)
theorem not_mixture_alkane : ¬ Alkane Mixture := by
  intro hA
  have hc : Chemical Mixture := alkane_to_chemical Mixture hA
  have nc : ¬ Chemical Mixture := not_chemical_mixture
  exact nc hc

axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, Alkane x → Hydrocarbon x
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Organic x → Chemical x
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Organic x → ContainsCarbon x
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, Chemical x → ¬ SingleElement x
axiom P6 : (Chemical Mixture ∧ SingleElement Mixture) ∨ (¬ Chemical Mixture ∧ ¬ SingleElement Mixture)

-- Chain: Alkane → Hydrocarbon → Organic → Chemical
theorem alkane_to_chemical (x : obj) (hA : Alkane x) : Chemical x := by
  have hH : Hydrocarbon x := P2 x hA
  have hO : Organic x := P1 x hH
  have hC : Chemical x := P3 x hO
  exact hC

-- From P5 and P6, Mixture is neither chemical nor single-element
theorem neither_mixture : ¬ Chemical Mixture ∧ ¬ SingleElement Mixture := by
  cases P6 with
  | inl hcs =>
      have contra : False := (P5 Mixture hcs.left) hcs.right
      exact False.elim contra
  | inr hne =>
      exact hne

theorem not_chemical_mixture : ¬ Chemical Mixture :=
  (neither_mixture).left

theorem not_singleelement_mixture : ¬ SingleElement Mixture :=
  (neither_mixture).right

-- Hence Mixture cannot be an alkane (since alkanes are chemicals)
theorem not_mixture_alkane : ¬ Alkane Mixture := by
  intro hA
  have hc : Chemical Mixture := alkane_to_chemical Mixture hA
  exact not_chemical_mixture hc

-- Therefore the target conjunction is false
theorem not_target : ¬ (Alkane Mixture ∧ ContainsCarbon Mixture) := by
  intro h
  exact not_mixture_alkane h.left

axiom BileDuctCancer : obj → Prop
axiom Cholangiocarcinoma : obj → Prop
axiom MildFlu : obj → Prop

axiom ColorectalCancer : obj

axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, DeadlyDisease x → LowSurvival x
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, SevereCancer x → DeadlyDisease x
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, BileDuctCancer x → SevereCancer x
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Cholangiocarcinoma x → BileDuctCancer x
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, MildFlu x → LowSurvival x
axiom P6 : ¬ (BileDuctCancer ColorectalCancer ∧ LowSurvival ColorectalCancer)

lemma bile_implies_low : ∀ x : obj, BileDuctCancer x → LowSurvival x :=
  fun x hb => P1 x (P2 x (P3 x hb))

theorem not_bile_colorectal : ¬ BileDuctCancer ColorectalCancer := by
  intro hb
  have hlow : LowSurvival ColorectalCancer := bile_implies_low ColorectalCancer hb
  exact P6 (And.intro hb hlow)

  intro hchol
  have hbile : BileDuctCancer ColorectalCancer := P4 ColorectalCancer hchol
  exact not_bile_colorectal hbile

  let P := (Cholangiocarcinoma ColorectalCancer ∧ (MildFlu ColorectalCancer ∨ BileDuctCancer ColorectalCancer))
  P ∨ P

-- We show the statement is false under the premises (hence it does not follow).

  intro hs
  have hPfalse :
      ¬ (Cholangiocarcinoma ColorectalCancer ∧ (MildFlu ColorectalCancer ∨ BileDuctCancer ColorectalCancer)) := by
    intro h

  cases hs with

  | inr h => exact hPfalse h

axiom Likes : obj → obj → Prop

-- Jane does not like any animals that bark.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, (Animal x ∧ Barks x) → ¬ Likes Jane x
-- All dogs are animals that bark.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Dog x → (Animal x ∧ Barks x)
-- Jane likes all animals that jump.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, (Animal x ∧ Jumps x) → Likes Jane x
-- If an animal has legs, then it jumps.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, (Animal x ∧ Legs x) → Jumps x
-- If an animal is terricolous, then it has legs.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, (Animal x ∧ Terricolous x) → Legs x
-- KiKi is an animal.
axiom A6 : Animal KiKi
-- If KiKi neither jumps nor has legs, then KiKi is terricolous or has legs.
axiom A7 : (¬ Jumps KiKi ∧ ¬ Legs KiKi) → (Terricolous KiKi ∨ Legs KiKi)

theorem kiki_not_bark_and_not_dog : (¬ Barks KiKi) ∧ (¬ Dog KiKi) := by
  classical
  -- First, derive that KiKi jumps or has legs (classically).
  have hJL : Jumps KiKi ∨ Legs KiKi := by
    by_contra hnot
    have hsplit : ¬ Jumps KiKi ∧ ¬ Legs KiKi := not_or.mp hnot
    have hTL : Terricolous KiKi ∨ Legs KiKi := A7 hsplit
    have hL : Legs KiKi :=
      Or.elim hTL
        (fun hT => A5 KiKi (And.intro A6 hT))
        (fun hL => hL)
    exact hnot (Or.inr hL)
  -- From that, conclude KiKi jumps.
  have hJ : Jumps KiKi :=
    Or.elim hJL
      (fun h => h)
      (fun hL => A4 KiKi (And.intro A6 hL))
  -- Therefore, Jane likes KiKi.
  have hLike : Likes Jane KiKi := A3 KiKi (And.intro A6 hJ)
  -- Hence KiKi does not bark.
  have hNotBark : ¬ Barks KiKi := by
    intro hb
    have hNotLike : ¬ Likes Jane KiKi := A1 KiKi (And.intro A6 hb)
    exact hNotLike hLike
  -- And KiKi is not a dog (since dogs bark).
  have hNotDog : ¬ Dog KiKi := by
    intro hd
    have hAB : Animal KiKi ∧ Barks KiKi := A2 KiKi hd
    exact hNotBark hAB.right
  exact And.intro hNotBark hNotDog
</lean>

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom AiltonSilva : obj
axiom Ailton : obj
axiom Braga : obj
axiom Nautico : obj
axiom Fluminense : obj

axiom FootballPlayer : obj → Prop
axiom BrazilianFootballer : obj → Prop
axiom PlaysFor : obj → obj → Prop
axiom LoanedTo : obj → obj → Prop
axiom FootballClub : obj → Prop

-- Ailton Silva is commonly known as Ailton (treat as the same person)
axiom alias : AiltonSilva = Ailton

-- Ailton was loaned out to Braga
axiom loaned_Ailton_Braga : LoanedTo Ailton Braga

-- Náutico and Braga (and Fluminense) are football clubs
axiom club_Nautico : FootballClub Nautico
axiom club_Braga : FootballClub Braga
axiom club_Fluminense : FootballClub Fluminense

-- Additional context (not needed for the proof but faithful to the text)
axiom fp_Ailton : FootballPlayer Ailton
axiom braz_AiltonSilva : BrazilianFootballer AiltonSilva
axiom plays_AiltonSilva_Nautico : PlaysFor AiltonSilva Nautico

theorem ailton_silva_loaned_to_football_club : ∃ c : obj, FootballClub c ∧ LoanedTo AiltonSilva c :=
by
  have h : LoanedTo AiltonSilva Braga := by
    simpa [alias.symm] using loaned_Ailton_Braga
  exact Exists.intro Braga (And.intro club_Braga h)
</lean>
